original_hypothesis.title: FeeRateModel.getFeeRate Unchecked Return Values Enable Swap DoS or Fee Extraction
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FeeRateModel.getFeeRate delegates to an external implementation contract without validating return values. If the implementation returns lpFeeRate + mtFeeRate > 1e18 (100%), the subtraction in querySellBase/querySellQuote (`receiveQuoteAmount - mulFloor(receiveQuoteAmount, lpFeeRate) - mtFee`) underflows and reverts, permanently DOSing all trading. Alternatively, a malicious implementation returning extremely high mtFeeRate could redirect nearly all swap output to the maintainer.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The FeeRateModel owner can change the implementation at any time. The deployment script sets FeeRateModelImpl which takes 50% of LP fees, but a future implementation change (or compromised owner) could set values that break the fee arithmetic in MagicLP's swap functions. There is no upper bound validation on returned fee rates.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: MIMSwap.s.sol sets FeeRateModelImpl; FeeRateModel.sol:setImplementation allows owner to change at any time; no return value validation in getFeeRate

proof_of_signal.pattern: getFeeRate returns raw values from external implementation; no bounds check on adjustedLpFeeRate or mtFeeRate; used directly in subtraction that can underflow

preconditions: Implementation returns fee rates where lpFeeRate + mtFeeRate > 1e18

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: receiveQuoteAmount - mulFloor(receiveQuoteAmount, lpFeeRate) - mtFee; underflows if lpFeeRate portion + mtFee > receiveQuoteAmount
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: FeeRateModel owner deploys malicious implementation → all pool swaps revert or route excessive fees to maintainer
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: With mtFeeRate = 99%, nearly all swap output goes to maintainer address

economic_questions: What fee rate values cause underflow in the swap calculation?
Can the FeeRateModel implementation be changed without timelock?
Does this affect all pools using the same FeeRateModel?
validation_requirements: Test querySellBase with lpFeeRate = 1e18 (100%) → should underflow
Verify setImplementation has no timelock or multi-sig requirement
Check if individual pools can override the FeeRateModel

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 100% of all swap volume across all affected pools
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Near 100% if extracting via inflated mtFeeRate
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Persistent until implementation is changed back

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Affects all pools using the compromised FeeRateModel

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All traders and LPs on pools using the affected FeeRateModel
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 100% of swap output redirected
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All trading volume across affected pools

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current FeeRateModelImpl takes divCeil(lpFeeRate, 2) as mtFee; max ~50%
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test with implementation returning (0, 2e18) → mtFee > receiveAmount → revert

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
